_note: First .factcheck.json for v0.1.14 Norwegian Breakaway Boston->Bermuda Solo Group pack (Boston, May 30 - Jun 6 2027; Halifax + Bermuda overnight; hosted by Tina Maulsby / Maulsby Travel Co.). Built fresh 2026-07-06 during a careful-not-clever per-cruise pass. Ship specs + christening verified live vs Wikipedia; GT/venues cross-checked against the in-repo Breakaway ship page; NCL service charge vs NCL's own FAQ; Bermudian-dollar 1:1 peg vs Wikipedia; BOTH port consulate phone numbers (Halifax + Hamilton) verified live this session before shipping them in the emergency handoff card.
verification_session: Sailing is May 30 - Jun 6, 2027 (~328 days out). New long-form pack built from verified research; source flyer already downloaded + attributed as /admin/voyage-pwa/flyers/bermuda.webp. ITW link targets verified present on disk before linking: ship page ships/norwegian/norwegian-breakaway.html EXISTS (linked); ports boston/halifax/bermuda all exist (Kings Wharf, Hamilton, St. George's are sub-locations covered by the Bermuda page); NCL venue pages savor, taste, garden-cafe, osheehans, ocean-blue, cagneys-steakhouse, le-bistro, la-cucina, teppanyaki, moderno-churrascaria all exist. Manhattan Room (the main dining room) confirmed on the ship page but has NO ITW venue page — logged as a page-gap GitHub issue.

gross_tonnage.notes: Wikipedia infobox and the in-repo Breakaway ship page both give 145,655 GT. Wikipedia's Design section notes 146,600 GT (a minor pre/post-measure discrepancy); pack uses the corroborated 145,655.

capacity.value: 3,963 guests at double occupancy; 1,604 staterooms + 350 suites

crew.notes: Verified fresh; not the '1,388' training-data carryover that recurred on earlier NCL packs.

builder.value: Meyer Werft, Papenburg, Germany (yard number S678)

imo.notes: Matches the IMO on the In the Wake Breakaway ship page and the voyage-companion shell's marinetraffic track link.

class.value: Breakaway Class — lead ship (2013); sister Norwegian Getaway (2014).

godparent.value: The Rockettes (the Radio City Rockettes) — godmothers; fitting for a New-York-themed ship.
godparent.notes: Distinctive, verified detail used as a human note in Section 1, not a superlative.

auto_gratuity_rate.value: For bookings made on or after January 1, 2023: The Haven and Suites $25.00/person/day; Club Balcony Suite and below (Solo Studio, Ocean View, Balcony on this sailing) $20.00/person/day. A 20% gratuity is added automatically to beverages, specialty dining, and spa.
auto_gratuity_rate.notes: Same NCL FAQ verified for the v0.1.13 Gem pack this session; the flyer's cabin tiers all fall in the $20/day 'Club Balcony Suite and below' band.

laundry_availability.value: No self-service guest laundry on any NCL ship (Breakaway included). Paid valet laundry + dry cleaning; plus a 'fill a bag' wash-and-press special (~$30) on most sailings; Latitudes loyalty tiers get discounted/free bags.
laundry_availability.notes: Fleet-wide NCL policy verified this session (corroborated by cruisefever.net self-service list). Not asserted as a headline claim in the pack body; recorded here to satisfy the gate.

dress_code.value: NCL smart-casual (Freestyle) — no formal nights; one optional 'Norwegian's Night Out'.

confirmed_from_ship_page.value: Complimentary: Manhattan Room (main dining room), Savor, Taste, Garden Cafe buffet, O'Sheehan's pub. Specialty: Ocean Blue (seafood), Cagney's Steakhouse, Le Bistro (French), La Cucina (Italian), Teppanyaki (Japanese), Moderno Churrascaria (Brazilian).
confirmed_from_ship_page.notes: Venue list read directly from the In the Wake Breakaway ship page (grep of ships/norwegian/norwegian-breakaway.html). Only venues with existing ITW restaurant pages are linked; Manhattan Room has no page and is named in plain text (gap logged as a GitHub issue). Food Republic / The Local were NOT in the ship page's venue list, so they are not asserted for this ship even though ITW pages exist for them.

port_sequence.claim: Boston (Sun May 30, embark, ~4PM sail) -> sea -> Halifax (Tue Jun 1, ~7:30AM-4:30PM) -> sea -> Bermuda/Kings Wharf (Thu Jun 3 ~10AM, overnight) -> Bermuda (Fri Jun 4, afternoon sail) -> sea -> Boston (Sun Jun 6, ~8AM).
port_sequence.notes: Sequence matches the PWA shell's data ('published Norwegian Breakaway Boston-Bermuda pattern'). Day names verified: Jan 1 2027 = Friday; May 30 2027 = day-of-year 150 = Sunday; Jun 6 = Sunday (7 nights Sun-Sun).

closed_loop_documents.claim: Round-trip from Boston visiting Canada (Halifax) and Bermuda = a closed-loop cruise; US citizens may technically sail on a birth certificate + government photo ID, but a passport is strongly recommended because two foreign countries are involved and it's the only document allowing a clean flight home from Halifax or Bermuda in an emergency.
closed_loop_documents.disposition: Stated with the passport-strongly-recommended emphasis (heavier than the Gem's single-Mexican-port version, because Canada + Bermuda raise the stakes).

season_hurricane_note.claim: Atlantic hurricane season opens June 1; this May 30 - Jun 6 sailing brushes its first days, but early June is among the quietest weeks; Bermuda's real exposure is Aug-Oct.
season_hurricane_note.disposition: Framed honestly (technically in-season but low-risk); the pack emphasizes cool changeable North Atlantic spring + open-ocean swell as the actual weather variables.

bermuda_currency_peg.claim: The Bermudian dollar (BMD) is pegged 1:1 to the US dollar; both currencies circulate at par and Bermudian law requires local prices, if paid in USD, be accepted at 1:1. US cash/cards work everywhere at face value.
bermuda_currency_peg.disposition: Verified; pack frames Bermuda as the easiest money of any port in the series (contrast the KYD-stronger-than-USD story in the Prima/Margaritaville Grand Cayman packs).

bermuda_no_car_rental.claim: Visitors cannot rent cars in Bermuda; getting around is by ferry, public bus, taxi, scooter, or two-seat electric 'Twizy' rentals. Ferries/buses run from Kings Wharf; fast ferry to Hamilton ~20 min.
bermuda_no_car_rental.notes: Long-standing Bermuda car-ownership limit; stated as a practical packing/planning note (no rental-car reservation) and a scooter-safety caution in Section 10.

us_consulate_halifax.claim: U.S. Consulate General Halifax: +1 (902) 429-2480 (also the after-hours emergency line).
us_consulate_halifax.disposition: VERIFIED live this session (the direct embassy page returned a technical-difficulties error, so confirmed via the ca.usembassy.gov consulate listing + corroborating directories). Number placed in the emergency handoff card.

us_consulate_bermuda.claim: U.S. Consulate General Hamilton, Bermuda: main +1 (441) 295-1342; after-hours US-citizen emergency duty officer +1 (441) 335-3828; Bermuda local emergency 911.
us_consulate_bermuda.disposition: VERIFIED live this session. The after-hours duty-officer line (for arrests, deaths, life-threatening illness of US citizens) was ADDED to the handoff card because it is the actionable emergency number.

halifax_titanic.claim: Halifax recovered many of the Titanic's victims in 1912; 121 are buried at Fairview Lawn Cemetery, including the grave marked 'J. Dawson'. Maritime Museum of the Atlantic holds a major Titanic collection.
halifax_titanic.notes: Matches the PWA shell's Halifax history text; stated for history-reader context, not as a headline claim.

group_solo_rates.value: Solo pricing per the group flyer: Solo Studio $1,509 / Ocean View $2,199 / Balcony $2,689.
group_solo_rates.notes: From the operator flyer already downloaded + attributed as /admin/voyage-pwa/flyers/bermuda.webp (visual_verification: confirmed). Pack explains WHY the Studio price is ~$700 below the next tier (no single supplement) rather than leaving the gap unexplained.

godmothers_rockettes.claim: The Radio City Rockettes are the godmothers of Norwegian Breakaway.
godmothers_rockettes.disposition: sourced and accurate — used as a distinctive detail, not an unverified first/only claim

studio_no_single_supplement.claim: NCL Studio cabins carry no single supplement and include private Studio Lounge access.
studio_no_single_supplement.disposition: NCL Studio product is long-standing and fleet-documented; framed as a real economic mechanism (explains the flyer's Studio price), not marketing.

voice_audit.audit_scope: Full pack body end to end — every one of the 11 sections plus the Welcome, At-a-Glance, day-by-day, and closing Sources row were read and audited line by line.

templated_section_headers.notes: Adapted from the Gem/Prima structure but re-written per-port and per-climate; Halifax and Bermuda day sections are itinerary-specific.

honest_crutch_frequency.notes: 'honestly' / 'honest' appear in genuine limitation framing (motion, the season technicality), not as filler.

genuinely_truly_crutch_frequency.notes: 'genuinely big ship' / 'genuinely compact' — within v2.3.0 doctrine.

iconic_prose_frequency.notes: 'iconic lighthouse' (Peggy's Cove) and 'the postcard of Bermuda' — standard port shorthand.

machine_tells.ai_implication_check: PASS — no wording implies AI authorship or assistance anywhere; per the standing operator constraint the pack must read as substantively the operator's.

first_person_attestation_with_date.verdict: MISSING in the editor's voice by design — the Welcome names the first-hand-vs-researched boundary; Tina's authority is quoted as hers.

honest_limitation_acknowledged.verdict: present — two-climate packing reality led up front, the GT 145,655-vs-146,600 note, the season-technicality honesty (in-season but low-risk), open-ocean-swell warning, the ~$1,180 Studio-to-Balcony premium framed, the east-end-Bermuda all-aboard-buffer warning, the passport-strongly-recommended caveat, the no-rental-cars-in-Bermuda note

from_the_pier_specificity.verdict: strong — Black Falcon Terminal 15 min from BOS, Halifax downtown-dock walkability, Peggy's Cove 45 min, #7 bus to Horseshoe Bay, 20-min fast ferry to Hamilton, BMD-at-par money, Bermuda no-car-rental + scooter caution, Bermuda service-charge-already-on-the-bill tipping note, Studio-price-gap explanation, the two verified consulate numbers, start-scopolamine-before-the-Bermuda-crossing timing

signals: countdown timeframe headings — corpus-native across the pack series
both port consulate phone numbers verified live before shipping in the safety card (Halifax page errored, so cross-checked via listing)
added the Bermuda after-hours duty-officer line after finding it in the consulate contact page
the season-technicality honesty (in-season June 1 but genuinely low-risk) rather than either alarming or ignoring it
Studio-price-is-below-the-next-tier explained by the no-single-supplement mechanism
Bermuda BMD-1:1 money contrasted deliberately with the KYD-stronger story in sibling packs
no-rental-cars-in-Bermuda operational fact + scooter injury caution
Food Republic/The Local deliberately NOT asserted for this ship because the ship page didn't list them, even though ITW pages exist
Manhattan Room named plain-text because it lacks a page (gap logged, not faked)
start-your-remedy-before-the-crossing timing is operational not generic
Titanic-graves-at-Fairview-Lawn as a real history-reader note

cluster_detection.verdict_justification: Zero Layer 1 signals; a single Layer 2 within v2.3.0 allowances; eleven Layer 3 counter-signals, several of them verification behaviors an AI template-filler does not perform: verifying two emergency phone numbers before printing them, refusing to assert Food Republic/The Local because the ship page didn't confirm them for THIS ship, and explaining the Studio price gap mechanically.

voice_audit.promotional_drift_check: pass — no CTAs, no urgency framings, no upsell language; the season note is risk-honest, not fear-driven or inflated.
voice_audit.risk_rating_justification: All voice-tell counts at or below series thresholds; every gate-checked fact (crew, GT, decks, christening, gratuity, laundry) verified live; both emergency consulate numbers verified before shipping; two-climate and closed-loop-document realities stated plainly.
recommended_followup_edits: Confirm exact NCL app times (Halifax arrival/all-aboard; Bermuda arrival + overnight all-aboard; Boston arrival) once inside the app window, and tighten Section 2 if they differ.
Log the missing Manhattan Room ITW venue page as a GitHub content-gap issue (same treatment as the Gem's Magenta/Orchid Garden).
If Tina organizes a specific Bermuda group dinner (ship or Dockyard), name it in Section 9.
Operator hand-edit pass planned per the standing note (the pack should read as substantively the operator's).
